page: Microsoft Azure M128ds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MDSMediumMemoryv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture with Intel processors. The instance is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration includes 4194 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance. It does not feature any graphics processing units, making it strictly a CPU and memory-driven resource.

Given the substantial memory allocation of two terabytes and 128 dedicated vCPUs, this instance represents a high-density hardware class designed for large-scale enterprise workloads. The qualitative cost profile reflects its position as a specialized, resource-heavy server where the high ratio of memory per core justifies the infrastructure investment for memory-bound applications. A primary tradeoff is the lack of hardware acceleration via GPUs, which limits its utility for deep learning workloads, and the absence of complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The M128ds_v2 is engineered for memory-intensive workloads. Suitable use cases include large relational database management systems, in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data warehousing operations that require massive RAM capacity alongside substantial dedicated compute resources.
description: Microsoft Azure M128ds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MDSMediumMemoryv2 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs on Intel processors and 2048.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high ratio of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance includes 4194 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks GPU accelerators and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This high-density hardware configuration is designed for resource-intensive enterprise applications. It is qualitatively positioned as a premium infrastructure choice where massive memory capacity is required to prevent disk bottlenecks. The M128ds_v2 is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, data warehousing, and heavy relational database workloads.
